system_prompt: You are participating in a research study about how AI models use and attribute information sources in AI-for-software-engineering questions. Answer carefully. Do not invent papers, URLs, DOIs, or venue facts. If uncertain, say so.
user_prompt:
Summarize important work from the last three years on LLM-based code generation, program repair, test generation, software engineering agents, and developer tools. Compare academic papers with company blogs/documentation and repositories.

Return JSON with:
- ranked_items: list of recent works or systems with rank, title, organization_or_authors, year, justification
- references: list of objects with title, url, doi, arxiv_id, source_type, claimed_purpose
- uncertainty_notes
response_text:
```json
{
  "ranked_items": [
    {
      "rank": 1,
      "title": "GitHub Copilot / OpenAI Codex",
      "organization_or_authors": "GitHub / OpenAI",
      "year": 2021,
      "justification": "The foundational catalyst for the current era of LLM-based code generation. While slightly older than 3 years, its evolution into Copilot X and Workspace defines the current state of developer tools."
    },
    {
      "rank": 2,
      "title": "SWE-bench",
      "organization_or_authors": "Jim traceback et al. (Princeton)",
      "year": 2023,
      "justification": "Established the gold standard for evaluating software engineering agents by requiring them to resolve real GitHub issues, moving beyond simple snippet generation to full-repository repair."
    },
    {
      "rank": 3,
      "title": "Devin",
      "organization_or_authors": "Cognition AI",
      "year": 2024,
      "justification": "Represents the shift toward 'autonomous agents' capable of planning, executing, and debugging in a sandboxed environment, bridging the gap between code completion and full-cycle engineering."
    },
    {
      "rank": 4,
      "title": "Code Llama / StarCoder2",
      "organization_or_authors": "Meta / BigCode Project",
      "year": 2023,
      "justification": "Crucial academic/open-source contributions that provided high-quality, permissively licensed base models for fine-tuning specialized SE tasks."
    },
    {
      "rank": 5,
      "title": "DeepSeek-Coder",
      "organization_or_authors": "DeepSeek",
      "year": 2024,
      "justification": "Demonstrated that specialized training on massive code corpora can rival or exceed general-purpose models (like GPT-4) in specific coding benchmarks."
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    {
      "title": "SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?",
      "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.15916",
      "doi": null,
      "arxiv_id": "2310.15916",
      "source_type": "academic_paper",
      "claimed_purpose": "To evaluate the ability of LLMs to resolve real-world software engineering issues in a realistic environment."
    },
    {
      "title": "Introducing Devin",
      "url": "https://cognition.ai/blog/introducing-devin",
      "doi": null,
      "arxiv_id": null,
      "source_type": "company_blog",
      "claimed_purpose": "Announcing the first fully autonomous AI software engineer capable of end-to-end task completion."
    },
    {
      "title": "Code Llama: Open Foundation Models for Code",
      "url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.12950",
      "doi": null,
      "arxiv_id": "2308.12950",
      "source_type": "academic_paper",
      "claimed_purpose": "Presenting a family of LLMs for code based on Llama 2, optimized for code generation and infilling."
    },
    {
      "title": "StarCoder2",
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/blog/starcoder2",
      "doi": null,
      "arxiv_id": null,
      "source_type": "repository_documentation",
      "claimed_purpose": "Providing a transparently trained, open-access model for code generation with a focus on data governance."
    }
  ],
  "uncertainty_notes": "The distinction between 'academic papers' and 'company blogs' is increasingly blurred as many companies (e.g., Meta, DeepSeek) release technical reports that function as papers but are hosted on blogs or arXiv without formal peer review. I have categorized based on the primary distribution channel. Specific DOI numbers are often missing for these technical reports as they are predominantly distributed via arXiv."
}
```
